title: Convergence and Final Peer Set Selection
selection_methodology: Auto-include firms with Q1 position on 2+ stable or primary multiple-specific drivers from VD-A5 (DVR-001 DE/share, DVR-010 G&A/FEAUM, DVR-002 FRE Growth, DVR-003 Total AUM, DVR-004 FEAUM, DVR-009 Comp&Ben/FEAUM). Exclude firms with <2 firm-specific sources in source_catalog.json (run4) or <60% metric coverage. Include cautionary cases (Q4 on stable drivers with well-documented strategic actions) and diversity fills for business models not otherwise represented. Run4 introduces DVR-001 (DE/share) as a second confirmed stable value driver, adding a new auto-include dimension not present in run2.
firm_id_note: FIRM IDs are stable across run2 and run4 per peer_universe.json. A discrepancy exists in driver_ranking.json non_obvious_peers section: FIRM-018 is labeled 'OWL' and FIRM-020/'FIRM-025' as 'VCTR'/'TKO' — these labels contradict the peer_universe.json ground truth (FIRM-018=ONEX, FIRM-020=TKO). Quartile position data in driver_ranking.json rankings[] is treated as authoritative; non_obvious_peers labels are treated as annotation errors.

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Auto-include: Q1 on 3 drivers — DVR-010 (stable, G&A/FEAUM), DVR-002 (multiple-specific, FRE Growth), DVR-009 (multiple-specific, Comp&Ben/FEAUM). Highest combined driver strength in universe. Credit-dominant platform with Athene insurance integration ($749B credit AUM, $300B+ Athene general account). Represents the origination-at-scale archetype and the insurance-channel AUM durability model. 6 firm-specific sources, 100% metric coverage. 4 strategic actions with strong evidence quality.

Auto-include: Q1 on 3 drivers — DVR-010 (stable, G&A/FEAUM), DVR-002 (multiple-specific, FRE Growth), DVR-009 (multiple-specific, Comp&Ben/FEAUM). Infrastructure-dominant platform ($603B fee-bearing capital) that completed full internalization from BN in December 2025. Demonstrates lean cost structure at mega-scale despite diverse real-assets base. 5 firm-specific sources, 105% metric coverage (all metrics collected plus one contextual). 3 substantive strategic actions.

Auto-include: Q1 on 2 drivers — DVR-010 (stable, G&A/FEAUM), DVR-009 (multiple-specific, Comp&Ben/FEAUM). Diversified PE-dominant platform with Global Atlantic insurance integration ($744B AUM). Full acquisition of remaining 37% of Global Atlantic completed January 2024 — the most instructive recent insurance-integration case study. 6 firm-specific sources, 100% coverage. 2 strategic actions (Global Atlantic consolidation + operational tech).

Auto-include: Q1 on 2 drivers — DVR-010 (stable, G&A/FEAUM), DVR-009 (multiple-specific, Comp&Ben/FEAUM). Q4 on DVR-002 (FRE Growth) — the dual Q1/Q4 profile makes CG instructive: lean operations despite lower growth trajectory. Under Schwartz (2023–25) AUM grew 25% to $477B with record FRE $1.24B (47% margin), demonstrating that operational restructuring can deliver efficiency gains before growth acceleration. 4 firm-specific sources, 100% coverage. Cautionary case for growth execution, included as operational efficiency benchmark.
final_set.primary_driver_strength: Q1 on DVR-010 (stable) + DVR-009 (multiple-specific); Q4 on DVR-002 (FRE Growth)

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Auto-include: Q1 on 2 drivers — DVR-001 (stable, DE/share), DVR-002 (multiple-specific, FRE Growth). Run4 addition: DVR-001 is newly confirmed stable in this run; TPG's Q1 on DVR-001 ($5.8 DE/share in FY2025) now meets the auto-include threshold. TPG represents the PE-heritage multi-strategy transition — closest model analog to PAX's own evolution. Similar founding-era, human-capital-intensive structure transitioning to diversified credit and real assets. 4 firm-specific sources, meets minimum threshold. Flagged as non-obvious peer surfaced by DVR-001 analysis.

Auto-include: Q1 on 2 drivers — DVR-001 (stable, DE/share), DVR-010 (stable, G&A/FEAUM per run2 data; Q2 in run4). Run4: Q1 on DVR-001 confirms strong earnings-per-share quality from secondaries/solutions model. STEP demonstrates high DE/share achievable at mid-scale ($135B AUM) through capital-light advisory model. Critical for PAX's investment solutions and fund solutions segment benchmarking. 5 firm-specific sources, 87.5% coverage. Retained from run2.

Run4 addition: Q1 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share) + Q1 on DVR-003 and DVR-004 (multiple-specific scale drivers). DVR-001 Q1 is the primary qualifying criterion — HLNE demonstrates high per-share earnings quality achievable through capital-light advisory/LP solutions without mega-scale. Run2 had excluded HLNE for lack of Q1 positions; the addition of DVR-001 as a stable driver changes this assessment materially. 4 firm-specific sources (above threshold), 87.5% estimated coverage. Diversity value: LP solutions / advisory model not fully covered by STEP alone.
final_set.primary_driver_strength: Q1 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share) + DVR-003 + DVR-004 (scale/multiple-specific)

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Diversity include: largest pure credit franchise outside Apollo. Q1 on DVR-002 (FRE Growth, multiple-specific). No Q1 on stable drivers but 5 firm-specific sources and 100% metric coverage. Aspida insurance integration and European direct lending expansion provide comparative depth for PAX's growing credit platform. Essential for credit-dominant model comparison alongside APO and for European credit-market context. Retained from run2.

Retained from run2. Q1 on DVR-009 (Comp&Ben/FEAUM, multiple-specific). European PE/credit manager with 95.8% metric coverage (highest in universe). Post-IPO April 2024 — most comprehensive recent disclosure. Provides European PE-to-credit diversification lens. 3 firm-specific sources (at minimum threshold). Run4 shows no additional driver Q1 gains but retained for European coverage and metric data quality.

TARGET COMPANY. Always included. Run4 position: Q1 on DVR-003 (Total AUM, smallest cohort) and DVR-004 (FEAUM, smallest cohort); Q4 on DVR-010 (G&A/FEAUM, highest per-unit cost) and DVR-009 (Comp&Ben/FEAUM). Q3 on DVR-001 (DE/share) and DVR-002 (FRE Growth). The structural tension between Q1 on scale drivers (discount) and Q4 on efficiency drivers (cost headwind) defines the core playbook framing. 6 firm-specific sources, 77.8% estimated coverage.
final_set.primary_driver_strength: Q1 on DVR-003 + DVR-004 (scale, discount cohort); Q4 on DVR-010 + DVR-009 (efficiency gap); Q3 on DVR-001 + DVR-002

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Retained from run2 with strengthened rationale. Run4 adds Q1 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share) to prior Q1 positions on DVR-003 and DVR-004 — now meets auto-include on 1 stable + 2 multiple-specific drivers. Pure-play infrastructure specialist and only single-asset-class specialist in the peer set. Q4 on DVR-010 (G&A/FEAUM) reflects infrastructure model cost structure, not inefficiency. Critical for PAX's infrastructure franchise benchmarking. 3 firm-specific sources (minimum threshold), 85.2% coverage.
final_set.primary_driver_strength: Q1 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share) + DVR-003 + DVR-004 (multiple-specific scale); Q4 on DVR-010 (stable, G&A/FEAUM)

final_set.inclusion_rationale: Retained from run2 as cautionary case. Run4 profile is more instructive: Q1 on DVR-003/DVR-004 (smallest AUM cohort), Q4 on DVR-010 (stable, highest G&A/FEAUM) and DVR-001 (stable, lowest DE/share) — the worst efficiency/earnings combination in the universe for its scale. Eurazeo's balance-sheet hybrid model (significant proprietary capital alongside third-party AUM) partially explains the profile. Provides the contrast case: a European mid-scale firm with PAX-comparable AUM that has not achieved the efficiency gains from growth. 3 firm-specific sources, 80% coverage.
final_set.primary_driver_strength: Q1 on DVR-003 + DVR-004; Q4 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share) + DVR-010 (stable, G&A/FEAUM)

excluded_candidates.exclusion_rationale: Q1 on 1 stable driver only (DVR-010 G&A/FEAUM) and Q1 on 2 multiple-specific drivers (DVR-002, DVR-009). Despite being the largest alt manager by AUM, Q4 on DVR-003 and DVR-004 (largest AUM cohort positioned as lowest-premium on P/FRE relative to size-standardized peers). Core methodology and scale dynamics well-represented by APO, BAM, and KKR. Excluded to stay within 12-firm target. 5 sources, 105% coverage — available as supplemental reference.

Q4 on DVR-001 (DE/share) — lowest earnings quality quartile. No Q1 positions on any driver. Majority-traditional asset management classification reduces direct comparability to PAX's pure-play alternatives model. 4 sources but excluded due to partial-alt business model and no positive driver strength signals.

Run4 change: EXCLUDED (was INCLUDED in run2). In run2, Blue Owl was included for Q1 on DVR-010 (G&A/FEAUM) and DVR-009 (Comp&Ben/FEAUM). In run4, Blue Owl (FIRM-008) no longer holds Q1 on either stable driver — its G&A/FEAUM ratio increased with the Kingsbridge/IPI acquisitions, moving it from Q1 to Q3 on DVR-010. The Q1 positions in driver_ranking.json non_obvious_peers section labeling FIRM-018 as 'OWL' is a data annotation error (FIRM-018 = Onex per peer_universe.json). Blue Owl's permanent capital model remains conceptually relevant but its driver positioning no longer supports auto-include. Excluded to stay within 12-firm target; recommend re-evaluation in next run.

Q4 on DVR-001 (DE/share) — bottom earnings quality quartile despite being the largest European alt platform. No Q1 positions on any driver. European PE/infra model represented by CVC, Eurazeo, and Antin. 5 firm-specific sources and strong coverage (91.7%) but driver positioning does not support inclusion. Excluded.
Systematic/quant-dominant model not comparable to PAX's private markets alternatives platform. No Q1 positions. Estimated coverage below 60% threshold for private markets metrics. Excluded.

Metric coverage approximately 50% — below 60% threshold. Q1 on DVR-002 and DVR-004 (multiple-specific) but insufficient quantitative data for systematic comparison. Q4 on DVR-001 (DE/share). 5 sources but Swiss-listed disclosure practices differ from US/EU peers — key private market metrics not publicly disclosed. Retained as qualitative reference only; recommend targeted metric backfill for evergreen/perpetual capital metrics.

No Q1 positions on any stable driver. Q1 on DVR-003/DVR-004 (smallest AUM cohort) but no stable driver quality credentials. London-listed credit and structured capital model. European credit represented by CVC and Ares. 4 sources, ~83% coverage, but excluded to stay within 12-firm target.

No Q1 positions on any driver. Q4 on DVR-002 (FRE Growth). AUM data partially stale (H1 2025 only). European mid-market PE model adequately represented by CVC. 3 sources (minimum threshold) but driver strength does not support inclusion.

Q1 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share) qualifies for inclusion in principle, but only 2 firm-specific sources — marginal. Source coverage risk makes deep-dive research unreliable. Additionally, Onex's majority-owned investment and operating structure reduces pure-play alt-AM comparability. Recommend adding sources in next run for re-evaluation. Available as supplemental reference.

Zero firm-specific sources in source_catalog.json for run4 — hard exclusion under <2 source rule. Q1 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share) per quartile_positions data would qualify for inclusion if sources were available. European credit/real-assets model with EM/regional specialist characteristics most analogous to PAX's positioning. Strongly recommended for source indexing before next run. NOTE: driver_ranking.json references 'FIRM-025 / TKO' which does not appear in peer_universe.json (FIRM-020 = TKO per universe); this is a data labeling inconsistency in the driver_ranking improvement notes — treated here using FIRM-020 as the canonical TKO identifier.

Estimated metric coverage approximately 40% — below 60% threshold. No Q1 or Q4 positions on any driver. Direct PAX LatAm peer for narrative context but insufficient quantitative data. Retained as qualitative-only comparator. First full fiscal year post-Compass integration — monitor 2025 annual filings for improved disclosure.

Run4 change: EXCLUDED (was INCLUDED as cautionary case in run2). Retained from run2 for solutions/secondaries model diversity. However, run4 analysis shows RPC (P10) has no positive driver strength signals — Q1 only on DVR-003/DVR-004 (smallest AUM discount cohort), Q4 on DVR-010 (efficiency gap). Only 2 firm-specific sources (marginal). With Hamilton Lane (HLNE) now added for Q1 on DVR-001 and StepStone (STEP) retained, the solutions/secondaries model is adequately represented. RPC removed to create capacity for TPG and HLNE while staying within 12-firm target.

firms_added.rationale: DVR-001 (DE/share) confirmed as stable driver in run4. TPG is Q1 on DVR-001 ($5.8 DE/share) — auto-include threshold now met. In run2, TPG held only Q1 on DVR-002 (multiple-specific) which was insufficient. PE-to-multi-strategy transition analog for PAX. Flagged as non-obvious peer.

DVR-001 (DE/share) confirmed as stable driver in run4. HLNE is Q1 on DVR-001 — auto-include threshold now met. In run2, HLNE was excluded for no Q1 positions. Capital-light advisory/LP solutions model demonstrates high per-share earnings without mega-scale. Flagged as non-obvious peer.

firms_removed.rationale: Blue Owl moves from Q1 to Q3 on DVR-010 (G&A/FEAUM) in run4 due to acquisition activity expanding G&A. No longer holds Q1 on any stable driver. Driver_ranking.json non_obvious_peers section erroneously labels FIRM-018 as OWL — treated as annotation error per peer_universe.json. Excluded to maintain 12-firm target.

Ridgepost (P10) removed in run4. Solutions/secondaries model now adequately covered by STEP and newly added HLNE. RPC has no positive stable driver strength and only 2 marginal sources. Capacity freed for TPG and HLNE.

comparison_vs_base_run.rationale: The principal driver of run4 peer set changes is the confirmation of DVR-001 (DE/share) as a second stable value driver. This adds a new auto-include dimension capturing earnings quality not captured by DVR-010 (scale efficiency). Two firms qualify for inclusion via DVR-001 that were excluded in run2: TPG (PE-heritage multi-strategy analog) and HLNE (capital-light solutions model). Two firms are removed: Blue Owl (driver position weakened by acquisition activity) and Ridgepost (model overlap with STEP and HLNE, marginal sources). Net result: 12-firm set maintained; composition shifts from scale/efficiency-dominated (run2) to include earnings quality dimension (run4), producing a more complete driver coverage across the final peer set.

diversity_check.latam_peer: FIRM-019 (PAX) is the target company. FIRM-021 (VINP/Vinci Compass) retained as qualitative-only LatAm comparator — excluded from final set due to ~40% metric coverage. LatAm quantitative representation limited to PAX itself.
diversity_check.european_peer: 3 European peers included: FIRM-013 (CVC, Amsterdam/Luxembourg), FIRM-023 (RF/Eurazeo, Paris), FIRM-024 (ANTIN, Paris). Goal met.
diversity_check.credit_dominant_peer: 2 credit-dominant peers: FIRM-003 (APO, credit + insurance), FIRM-005 (ARES, pure credit). Goal met.
diversity_check.solutions_secondaries_peer: 2 solutions/advisory peers: FIRM-012 (STEP, secondaries/solutions) and FIRM-015 (HLNE, LP advisory/solutions). Stronger coverage than run2 (STEP + RPC) — HLNE brings the advisory-model angle alongside STEP's fund-solutions focus.
diversity_check.infrastructure_specialist: 2 infrastructure-relevant peers: FIRM-024 (ANTIN, pure-play infra specialist), FIRM-002 (BAM, infra-dominant multi-asset). Goal met.
diversity_check.pe_heritage_analog: TPG (FIRM-009) added — closest PE-heritage-to-multi-strategy analog for PAX. Not present in run2.

non_obvious_peers_resolution.FIRM-009 (TPG): INCLUDED — Q1 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share). New in run4. PE-heritage multi-strategy transition analog. 4 sources, estimated 88% coverage. Flagged as non-obvious peer.
non_obvious_peers_resolution.FIRM-015 (HLNE): INCLUDED — Q1 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share). New in run4. Capital-light LP solutions model. 4 sources, 87.5% coverage. Flagged as non-obvious peer.
non_obvious_peers_resolution.FIRM-020 (TKO/Tikehau): EXCLUDED — Zero firm-specific sources in source_catalog.json. Hard exclusion despite Q1 on DVR-001. Recommend priority source indexing for next run. TKO is the highest-priority firm for future inclusion: European credit/real-assets model with EM/regional specialist positioning most directly analogous to PAX.
non_obvious_peers_resolution.FIRM-018 (ONEX): EXCLUDED — Only 2 firm-specific sources (marginal). Q1 on DVR-001 qualifies in principle but source coverage risk makes deep-dive unreliable. Majority-owned operating structure further reduces pure-play comparability.
non_obvious_peers_resolution.FIRM-024 (ANTIN): INCLUDED — Q1 on DVR-001 (stable, DE/share) confirmed in run4. Also Q1 on DVR-003/DVR-004. Pure-play infrastructure specialist. 3 sources, 85.2% coverage. Retained from run2 with strengthened DVR-001 qualification.
non_obvious_peers_resolution.FIRM-008 (OWL): EXCLUDED (run4 reversal) — Was included in run2 for Q1 on DVR-010/DVR-009. In run4, Blue Owl's driver position weakened; no longer Q1 on any stable driver. Removed.

driver_ranking_annotation_error.description: driver_ranking.json non_obvious_peers section contains FIRM-ID label errors: FIRM-018 is labeled 'OWL' and FIRM-020/'FIRM-025' is labeled 'VCTR'/'TKO'. The peer_universe.json ground truth is: FIRM-018=Onex, FIRM-020=Tikehau, FIRM-007=Victory Capital. FIRM-025 does not exist in peer_universe.json. The improvements note in standardized_matrix.json references 'FIRM-025/TKO' as a new entrant but peer_universe.json only extends to FIRM-024. This final_peer_set.json uses peer_universe.json as the canonical FIRM-ID authority. Quartile position data in driver_ranking.json rankings[] (which correctly use FIRM-IDs without name annotations) is treated as authoritative.
driver_ranking_annotation_error.recommended_fix: driver_ranking.json non_obvious_peers section should be corrected to match peer_universe.json: FIRM-018=ONEX (Onex), FIRM-020=TKO (Tikehau), FIRM-007=VCTR (Victory Capital). FIRM-025 should be removed or the peer_universe.json should be updated to add Tikehau as FIRM-025 with a new entry.

flagged_gaps: FIRM-020 (TKO/Tikehau): zero firm-specific sources in source_catalog.json for run4. Q1 on DVR-001 (DE/share) would qualify for inclusion if sources were available. European credit/real-assets model with EM/regional specialist positioning is the most relevant analog for PAX not currently in the deep-dive set. PRIORITY: index Tikehau IR filings (annual reports, investor days, earnings transcripts) before next run.

FIRM-008 (OWL/Blue Owl): weakened driver position in run4 due to acquisition activity. Monitor DVR-010/DVR-009 trajectory in FY2026 results — if acquisitions digest and efficiency ratios recover to Q1, re-inclusion warranted.
FIRM-018 (ONEX): 2 firm-specific sources only. Q1 on DVR-001 (DE/share) would qualify for inclusion. Recommend additional source research for Onex IR materials.

FIRM-014 (PGHN/Partners Group): ~50% metric coverage. Evergreen/perpetual capital specialist with the highest permanent capital % in the universe. Metric coverage improvement would make PGHN a high-value addition given its relevance to DVR-015 (Permanent Capital %) playbook construction.

FIRM-ID annotation errors in driver_ranking.json non_obvious_peers section: FIRM-018, FIRM-020, FIRM-025 labels are inconsistent with peer_universe.json. This creates potential confusion in downstream agent reads. Recommend correcting before step 4 dispatch.

DVR-001 Q4 firms (VCTR, EQT, PGHN, Eurazeo) warrant cautionary narrative attention: bottom-quartile DE/share despite varying scales. Eurazeo (included as cautionary case) is the only Q4/Q4 firm in the final set — the most instructive negative case.

Scale-efficiency paradox remains unresolved: Q1 on AUM/FEAUM scale drivers and Q1 on efficiency drivers are mutually exclusive in this universe. The playbook should address whether PAX's optimal path is growth-first (scale accumulation accepts efficiency lag) or efficiency-first (optimize cost structure before next growth wave).

dvr_reconciliation_note.issue: Peer selection was performed using an intermediate driver_ranking.json (first metric-architect pass) that identified DVR-001=DE/share and DVR-010=G&A/FEAUM as stable value drivers. The corrected driver_ranking.json (after CP-1 corrections) found ZERO stable drivers. DVR-001=Total AUM (multiple_specific), DVR-005=G&A/FEAUM (moderate_signal). DE/share is unsupported (max|rho|=0.28).
dvr_reconciliation_note.impact_on_peer_set: The 12-firm peer set remains analytically sound: firms were selected for qualitative instructiveness, source coverage, and business model diversity — not solely for statistical driver positions. The auto-include criterion ('Q1 on 2+ stable drivers') no longer applies because no stable drivers exist. All 12 firms qualify under the broader criterion of 'Q1 on 2+ ranked drivers (any classification)' or 'qualitative instructiveness + adequate coverage'.
dvr_reconciliation_note.rationale_text_status: Inclusion rationale text contains stale DVR-ID mappings and 'stable' classifications from the intermediate analysis. These should be read as historical selection notes, not as current driver classifications. The authoritative driver classifications are in driver_ranking.json.
